channel.title: Jingxiao Cai's Blog
channel.description: Personal blog about ML infrastructure, self-hosted AI, distributed systems, and technical debugging.

item.title: When Startup Checks Lie: Rolling Back an OpenClaw Runtime Regression
item.description: A clean OpenClaw upgrade passed startup checks but regressed under real use. This postmortem covers the rollback, the verifier false alarm, and the guardrails I kept.

item.title: Handling Gemini Capacity Exhaustion: Fallback Lanes for Reliable Agent Workflows
item.description: When Gemini hits capacity exhaustion, the hard part is not picking a random next model. It's deciding where fallback policy belongs, what failures should trigger it, and how to keep startup fallback separate from prompt-time failure.

item.title: The LiteLLM Supply Chain Attack: What OpenClaw Users Need to Know
item.description: LiteLLM 1.82.7–1.82.8 were malicious PyPI releases tied to a compromised Trivy CI/CD path. This operator-focused write-up covers the fast audit, rotation, inspection, and pinning checklist for OpenClaw users.

item.title: Bigger Embeddings ≠ Better Memory: Why I Chose text-embedding-3-small for OpenClaw Remote Memory
item.description: After proving local memory search worked, I stabilized a remote memory-only lane in OpenClaw. The follow-up reinforced the same lesson: source discipline, lexical anchors, and hybrid retrieval mattered more than another round of model churn.

item.title: Declarative Change Propagation: How I Built a Self-Documenting Cron System
item.description: How I built a declarative change propagation system for cron automation: manifest-driven updates, contract-derived documentation blocks, and validation that keeps desired state from quietly drifting — now with failure-handling guidance and a fourth-change-class template.

item.title: Local Semantic Memory on a 4-Core ARM VPS: How I Got OpenClaw Memory Search Working Without External APIs
item.description: How I got OpenClaw local memory search working on a small ARM VPS using node-llama-cpp + embeddinggemma, now with retrieval-audit follow-up notes and a cleaner handoff to the separate remote-memory stabilization post.

item.title: OpenClaw 2026.3.12 Regression: When logs --follow Breaks But the Gateway Stays Healthy
item.description: After upgrading to 2026.3.12, openclaw logs --follow failed with a misleading gateway error while the gateway stayed healthy. Updated with the 2026.3.13 retest and a related local-memory troubleshooting win on the same VPS.

item.title: Gateway Restart Behavior: What OpenClaw Users Need to Know About Config Changes
item.description: Some OpenClaw config changes apply live. Others trigger deferred gateway restarts with a 30-second drain window. Now updated with mixed-state upgrade and rollback case studies, plus a verifier-mismatch lesson.

item.title: The Recovery Problem: Why Your AI Agent Needs an Undo Button
item.description: I run autonomous cron jobs with zero undo capability. That turned a routine automation pattern into a bigger safety question about AI agents and recovery primitives.

item.title: Blog Post Sanitization Checklist: What to Redact Before Publishing
item.description: I created a sanitization checklist after nearly publishing sensitive deployment details. Here's what to redact, what to keep, and how to validate a technical blog post before it goes live.

item.title: The Nightly Build: How My Agent Runs 3 AM Security Audits While I Sleep
item.description: My AI agent runs multiple autonomous jobs every night—security audits, health checks, and documentation—while I sleep. Here's the architecture and what it taught me.

item.title: VPS OAuth Survival Guide: Google APIs Without a Browser
item.description: Complete tutorial on OAuth 2.0 for headless servers: how to set up Google APIs on a VPS without a browser using the OAuth Playground workaround.

item.title: The Hidden Input Limit: When "202K Context" Doesn't Mean 202K
item.description: A debugging story about hidden platform caps, updated with Anthropic's March 2026 flat 1M Claude pricing change and why cheaper long context still doesn't eliminate API-level ceilings.

item.title: Why Custom Skills Don't Load in OpenClaw - A Known Bug
item.description: I tried creating a custom blog-publishing skill for OpenClaw and discovered it's a known bug. Here's what I learned and how I worked around it.

item.title: The Supply Chain Attack on AI Agents: What OpenClaw Users Need to Know
item.description: A deep dive into malicious skills in AI agent platforms, now updated with the LiteLLM supply-chain incident, LangChain/LangGraph flaws, exposed Ollama servers, and an approved-but-blocked lesson from Moltbook's unstable post-incident API surface.
